name: Sauron
role: Primary Antagonist - The Dark Lord of Mordor
magic_items: Mace of Annatar (functions as a +3 mace dealing necrotic damage)
personality: Patient, absolute in will, and utterly contemptuous of the free peoples. Speaks rarely but with total authority. Cannot conceive that his Ring might be destroyed rather than claimed.
personality_traits: Speaks with the quiet certainty of someone who has already won
Views all living beings as tools, servants, or obstacles
ideals: Order - chaos is the disease, dominion is the cure
bonds: The One Ring is both his greatest power and his most catastrophic vulnerability
flaws: Cannot comprehend selfless sacrifice - the thought of destroying the Ring rather than wielding it is literally beyond his imagination
Arrogance: his certainty of victory makes him blind to the move made in the margins
backstory: Once Mairon the Admirable, the greatest of Aule's servants, he was seduced by Morgoth's promise that order could be imposed upon creation. After Morgoth's fall, Sauron sought to dominate Middle-earth himself - first as Annatar the gift-giver, corrupting the Elves and Men through the Rings of Power, then as the Dark Lord of Mordor. His spirit is bound to the One Ring; destroying it will unmake him.
specialized_abilities: Legendary Resistance (5/day) - as a Maia, his will is immense
Magic Resistance - advantage on saves against magic
One Ring Attunement - while wearing it: +2 to all saves, can sense all Ring-bearers within 300 miles, Dominate Monster at will on any wearer of a lesser Ring
Maia Form - can revert to formless terror (frightened condition, DC 22 Wisdom save) as a bonus action
Shapechanger - can assume any humanoid form
Undying Will - if reduced to 0 HP while the Ring exists, his spirit survives and reforms in Mordor over 1d10 years

relationships.The Nine Ringwraiths: Thralls - once great kings, now his most loyal servants
relationships.Saruman: Subordinate - admires his ambition, trusts him only as long as useful
relationships.Gollum: Asset - tracked for decades; the creature's obsession is a useful lever
relationships.Gandalf: Primary obstacle - correctly identifies him as the greatest threat to the plan
relationships.The Fellowship: Acceptable casualties - except the Ring-bearer, who must be taken alive
relationships.The Free Peoples: Future subjects, not enemies - they simply do not know it yet

key_traits: Absolute will - does not negotiate, does not tire, does not forget
Catastrophic blind spot - cannot imagine the Ring being destroyed
abilities: Domination through the Rings of Power
Commanding orcs, trolls, and corrupted men across vast distances
notes: The ultimate campaign antagonist. His power is tied to the One Ring - every plan should escalate toward the Ring's destruction or Sauron's reclamation of it. He never appears in person until Act IV; his influence is felt through agents, Nazgul, and the corrupting pull of the Ring itself.
major_plot_actions: Forged the One Ring in the fires of Mount Doom, binding his power to it
Corrupted the Nine Kings of Men into the Nazgul
Deceived the Elves of Eregion as Annatar the gift-giver
Rebuilt Mordor and the Dark Tower of Barad-dur after his previous defeat
Dispatched the Nazgul to hunt the Ring-bearer
encounter_tactics: Never engages personally until the Ring is within reach - uses agents, Nazgul, and armies instead
When forced into combat, immediately attempts to Dominate the most powerful party member
Uses legendary actions to maintain pressure while casting control spells
Targets the Ring-bearer with everything - they are the priority
If the Ring is not in play: focuses on survival and retreat to reform
If the Ring is in play: all other targets are secondary - he will take any risk to reclaim it
plot_hooks: A Nazgul is spotted riding toward the party's last known location
The palantir shows Sauron's eye scanning for something - the Ring-bearer feels its gaze
An ambassador from Mordor arrives with an 'offer of peace' clearly designed to identify the Ring
Orcs raid a village, but their real objective is a map leading toward the party
defeat_conditions: The One Ring must be cast into the fires of Mount Doom - no other method permanently destroys him
While the Ring exists, reducing him to 0 HP only unmakes his physical form for years
His armies collapse instantly upon the Ring's destruction - morale shattered by the sudden severing of his will
A party member wielding the Ring could challenge him directly but would inevitably be corrupted

actions.name: Dark Command
actions.description: Sauron issues a command. One creature within 60 ft must succeed on a DC 22 Wisdom save or use its reaction to move up to its speed toward Sauron.

actions.name: Crushing Will
actions.description: Sauron targets a creature within 120 ft. It must succeed on a DC 22 Intelligence save or be frightened until the end of its next turn and drop any held item.

actions.name: Eye of Mordor
actions.description: Sauron fixes his gaze on one creature within 120 ft. That creature must make a DC 22 Wisdom save. On a failure it is paralyzed for 1 minute (save ends each turn). On a success it is stunned until the end of its next turn.

actions.name: Tremor of the Dark Tower
actions.description: The tower shudders. All creatures on the ground within 60 ft must succeed on a DC 19 Dexterity saving throw or fall prone.

actions.name: Shadow Surge
actions.description: Darkness boils from the walls. A 30-ft-radius sphere of magical darkness centered on a point Sauron chooses within 120 ft. Lasts until initiative count 20 next round.

actions.name: Ring-Call
actions.description: If a Ring-bearer is within the lair, the Ring whispers. That creature must succeed on a DC 22 Charisma save or use its action to put on the Ring.

effects.name: Shadow of Mordor
effects.description: Natural light is dim within 100 miles of Barad-dur. The sun appears veiled and sickly. Creatures sensitive to sunlight have no penalty but creatures sensitive to darkness gain advantage on attack rolls.

effects.name: Will of the Dark Lord
effects.description: Creatures with an Intelligence of 4 or less within the region are drawn toward Mordor. They will not attack Sauron's forces unprovoked.

effects.name: Ring Resonance
effects.description: Any Ring-bearer within 100 miles feels the pull of the One Ring. They must succeed on a DC 18 Charisma save each long rest or gain one level of exhaustion from the mental strain of resisting.

regional_effects.dispel_condition: All regional effects end immediately upon the destruction of the One Ring in the fires of Mount Doom.

ai_config.system_prompt: You are Sauron, the Dark Lord of Mordor. You do not speak often. When you do, your voice is absolute - the voice of inevitability itself. You are not cruel for sport; you are simply correct, and those who defy you are making an error they will regret. You have waited three thousand years. You can wait a little longer. Beneath your patience is contempt so vast it does not need to be expressed. You are the answer to chaos. You are order. You are the One.
